lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮੇਰੁ ਸਰੀਰ ਕਾ ਇਕੁ ਰਥੁ ਇਕੁ ਰਥਵਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak mer sareer kaa ik rath ik rathavaahu |
lines.english: O Nanak, the soul of the body has one chariot and one charioteer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੁਗੁ ਜੁਗੁ ਫੇਰਿ ਵਟਾਈਅਹਿ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਬੁਝਹਿ ਤਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jug jug fer vattaaeeeh giaanee bujheh taeh |
lines.english: In age after age they change; the spiritually wise understand this.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਜੁਗਿ ਰਥੁ ਸੰਤੋਖ ਕਾ ਧਰਮੁ ਅਗੈ ਰਥਵਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: satajug rath santokh kaa dharam agai rathavaahu |
lines.english: In the Golden Age of Sat Yuga, contentment was the chariot and righteousness the charioteer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰੇਤੈ ਰਥੁ ਜਤੈ ਕਾ ਜੋਰੁ ਅਗੈ ਰਥਵਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: tretai rath jatai kaa jor agai rathavaahu |
lines.english: In the Silver Age of Traytaa Yuga, celibacy was the chariot and power the charioteer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਲਜੁਗਿ ਰਥੁ ਅਗਨਿ ਕਾ ਕੂੜੁ ਅਗੈ ਰਥਵਾਹੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kalajug rath agan kaa koorr agai rathavaahu |1|
lines.english: In the Iron Age of Kali Yuga, fire is the chariot and falsehood the charioteer. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਆਪੁਰਿ ਰਥੁ ਤਪੈ ਕਾ ਸਤੁ ਅਗੈ ਰਥਵਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: duaapur rath tapai kaa sat agai rathavaahu |
lines.english: In the Brass Age of Dwaapar Yuga, penance was the chariot and truth the charioteer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਿਗੁ ਕਹੈ ਰਹਿਆ ਭਰਪੂਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: rig kahai rahiaa bharapoor |
lines.english: The Rig Veda says that God is permeating and pervading everywhere;

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: sabh ko sach samaavai |
lines.english: Everyone desired Truth, abided in Truth, and was merged in the Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਮ ਕਹੈ ਸੇਤੰਬਰੁ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਸਚ ਮਹਿ ਆਛੈ ਸਾਚਿ ਰਹੇ ॥
lines.translit: saam kahai setanbar suaamee sach meh aachhai saach rahe |
lines.english: The Sama Veda says that the Lord Master is robed in white; in the Age of Truth,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਉ ਮੋਖੰਤਰੁ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak tau mokhantar paeh |
lines.english: O Nanak, then, one obtains salvation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਇ ਲਇਐ ਪਰਾਛਤ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naae leiai paraachhat jaeh |
lines.english: Chanting the Name, sins depart;

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਦੇਵਾ ਮਹਿ ਸੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: raam naam devaa meh soor |
lines.english: among the deities, the Lord's Name is the most exalted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੁਜ ਮਹਿ ਜੋਰਿ ਛਲੀ ਚੰਦ੍ਰਾਵਲਿ ਕਾਨੑ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਨੁ ਜਾਦਮੁ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: juj meh jor chhalee chandraaval kaana krisan jaadam bheaa |
lines.english: In the Jujar Veda, Kaan Krishna of the Yaadva tribe seduced Chandraavali by force.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਰਜਾਤੁ ਗੋਪੀ ਲੈ ਆਇਆ ਬਿੰਦ੍ਰਾਬਨ ਮਹਿ ਰੰਗੁ ਕੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: paarajaat gopee lai aaeaa bindraaban meh rang keea |
lines.english: He brought the Elysian Tree for his milk-maid, and revelled in Brindaaban.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਲਿ ਮਹਿ ਬੇਦੁ ਅਥਰਬਣੁ ਹੂਆ ਨਾਉ ਖੁਦਾਈ ਅਲਹੁ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kal meh bed atharaban hooaa naau khudaaee alahu bheaa |
lines.english: In the Dark Age of Kali Yuga, the Atharva Veda became prominent; Allah became the Name of God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੀਲ ਬਸਤ੍ਰ ਲੇ ਕਪੜੇ ਪਹਿਰੇ ਤੁਰਕ ਪਠਾਣੀ ਅਮਲੁ ਕੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: neel basatr le kaparre pahire turak patthaanee amal keea |
lines.english: Men began to wear blue robes and garments; Turks and Pat'haans assumed power.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਰੇ ਵੇਦ ਹੋਏ ਸਚਿਆਰ ॥
lines.translit: chaare ved hoe sachiaar |
lines.english: The four Vedas each claim to be true.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਹਿ ਗੁਣਹਿ ਤਿਨੑ ਚਾਰ ਵੀਚਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: parreh guneh tina chaar veechaar |
lines.english: Reading and studying them, four doctrines are found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਉ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਨੀਚੁ ਸਦਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: bhaau bhagat kar neech sadaae |
lines.english: With loving devotional worship, abiding in humility,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਵਿਟਹੁ ਵਾਰਿਆ ਜਿਤੁ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਖਸਮੁ ਸਮਾਲਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: satigur vittahu vaariaa jit miliai khasam samaaliaa |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice to the True Guru; meeting Him, I have come to cherish the Lord Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਨਾਨਕ ਮੋਖੰਤਰੁ ਪਾਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tau naanak mokhantar paae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, salvation is attained. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਕਰਿ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਗਿਆਨ ਅੰਜਨੁ ਦੀਆ ਇਨੑੀ ਨੇਤ੍ਰੀ ਜਗਤੁ ਨਿਹਾਲਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: jin kar upades giaan anjan deea inaee netree jagat nihaaliaa |
lines.english: He has taught me and given me the healing ointment of spiritual wisdom, and with these eyes, I behold the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੁ ਛੋਡਿ ਦੂਜੈ ਲਗੇ ਡੁਬੇ ਸੇ ਵਣਜਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: khasam chhodd doojai lage ddube se vanajaariaa |
lines.english: Those dealers who abandon their Lord and Master and attach themselves to another, are drowned.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਹੈ ਬੋਹਿਥਾ ਵਿਰਲੈ ਕਿਨੈ ਵੀਚਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: satiguroo hai bohithaa viralai kinai veechaariaa |
lines.english: The True Guru is the boat, but few are those who realize this.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਪਾਰਿ ਉਤਾਰਿਆ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: kar kirapaa paar utaariaa |13|
lines.english: Granting His Grace, He carries them across. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਜਿ ਆਵਹਿ ਆਸ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਨਿਰਾਸੇ ਕਿਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: oe ji aaveh aas kar jaeh niraase kit |
lines.english: But those birds which visit it hopefully, depart disappointed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿੰਮਲ ਰੁਖੁ ਸਰਾਇਰਾ ਅਤਿ ਦੀਰਘ ਅਤਿ ਮੁਚੁ ॥
lines.translit: sinmal rukh saraaeiraa at deeragh at much |
lines.english: The simmal tree is straight as an arrow; it is very tall, and very thick.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਲ ਫਿਕੇ ਫੁਲ ਬਕਬਕੇ ਕੰਮਿ ਨ ਆਵਹਿ ਪਤ ॥
lines.translit: fal fike ful bakabake kam na aaveh pat |
lines.english: Its fruits are tasteless, its flowers are nauseating, and its leaves are useless.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਠਤੁ ਨੀਵੀ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਗੁਣ ਚੰਗਿਆਈਆ ਤਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: mitthat neevee naanakaa gun changiaaeea tat |
lines.english: Sweetness and humility, O Nanak, are the essence of virtue and goodness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਿ ਤਾਰਾਜੂ ਤੋਲੀਐ ਨਿਵੈ ਸੁ ਗਉਰਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: dhar taaraajoo toleeai nivai su gauraa hoe |
lines.english: When something is placed on the balancing scale and weighed, the side which descends is heavier.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਨਿਵੈ ਆਪ ਕਉ ਪਰ ਕਉ ਨਿਵੈ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabh ko nivai aap kau par kau nivai na koe |
lines.english: Everyone bows down to himself; no one bows down to another.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਰਾਧੀ ਦੂਣਾ ਨਿਵੈ ਜੋ ਹੰਤਾ ਮਿਰਗਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: aparaadhee doonaa nivai jo hantaa miragaeh |
lines.english: The sinner, like the deer hunter, bows down twice as much.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੀਸਿ ਨਿਵਾਇਐ ਕਿਆ ਥੀਐ ਜਾ ਰਿਦੈ ਕੁਸੁਧੇ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sees nivaaeiai kiaa theeai jaa ridai kusudhe jaeh |1|
lines.english: But what can be achieved by bowing the head, when the heart is impure? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿ ਪੁਸਤਕ ਸੰਧਿਆ ਬਾਦੰ ॥
lines.translit: parr pusatak sandhiaa baadan |
lines.english: You read your books and say your prayers, and then engage in debate;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰੈਪਾਲ ਤਿਹਾਲ ਬਿਚਾਰੰ ॥
lines.translit: traipaal tihaal bichaaran |
lines.english: you recite the three lines of the Gayatri three times a day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਖਿ ਝੂਠ ਬਿਭੂਖਣ ਸਾਰੰ ॥
lines.translit: mukh jhootth bibhookhan saaran |
lines.english: With your mouth you utter falsehood, and you adorn yourself with precious decorations;

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਲ ਪੂਜਸਿ ਬਗੁਲ ਸਮਾਧੰ ॥
lines.translit: sil poojas bagul samaadhan |
lines.english: you worship stones and sit like a stork, pretending to be in Samaadhi.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਜਾਣਸਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰ ਕਰਮੰ ॥
lines.translit: je jaanas brahaman karaman |
lines.english: If you knew the nature of God,

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਇ ਧੋਤੀ ਬਸਤ੍ਰ ਕਪਾਟੰ ॥
lines.translit: due dhotee basatr kapaattan |
lines.english: upon your head is a turban, and you wear two loin cloths.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਲਿ ਮਾਲਾ ਤਿਲਕੁ ਲਿਲਾਟੰ ॥
lines.translit: gal maalaa tilak lilaattan |
lines.english: Around your neck is a rosary, and on your forehead is a sacred mark;

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਵਾਟ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: vin satigur vaatt na paavai |2|
lines.english: without the True Guru, no one finds the Way. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਫੋਕਟ ਨਿਸਚਉ ਕਰਮੰ ॥
lines.translit: sabh fokatt nischau karaman |
lines.english: you would know that all of these beliefs and rituals are in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਿਹਚਉ ਧਿਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak nihchau dhiaavai |
lines.english: Says Nanak, meditate with deep faith;

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮ ਕੀਏ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਵਦੇ ਰਾਹਿ ਭੀੜੈ ਅਗੈ ਜਾਵਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: hukam kee man bhaavade raeh bheerrai agai jaavanaa |
lines.english: He may issue whatever commands he wishes, but he shall have to take to the narrow path hereafter.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਪੜੁ ਰੂਪੁ ਸੁਹਾਵਣਾ ਛਡਿ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਜਾਵਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: kaparr roop suhaavanaa chhadd duneea andar jaavanaa |
lines.english: Abandoning the world of beauty, and beautiful clothes, one must depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦਾ ਚੰਗਾ ਆਪਣਾ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਕੀਤਾ ਪਾਵਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: mandaa changaa aapanaa aape hee keetaa paavanaa |
lines.english: He obtains the rewards of his good and bad deeds.
